steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best describes the overall structure of the passage. Map the passage's moves: it presents a study and its findings, raises one possible explanation for the results, then rules that explanation out.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'two separate studies are compared': The passage discusses one study, not two. ✗
  • Choice B — 'a study is introduced, then a possible explanation is considered and rejected': This matches exactly — the study and its findings come first, then the possible explanation (varying access to nonpark amenities) is raised and then dismissed. ✓
  • Choice C — 'findings are attributed to a methodological flaw': The passage doesn't criticize the study's methodology. ✗
  • Choice D — 'an initial question is posed, then answered': The passage doesn't open with an explicit question — it presents findings and tests an explanation. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
